title: Amgen Inc. (AMGN) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: Opus 4.6 independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Amgen Inc. (AMGN). Economic Prospect Score: 60/100 — Moderate Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y10)
fcfGrowth.rationale: Amgen Inc. is projected to grow FCF at 6% annually based on competitive dynamics and historical performance.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 8.5% WACC reflects Amgen Inc.'s risk profile, sector volatility, and balance sheet quality.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.5% terminal rate for long-run growth convergence toward nominal GDP.

thesisParagraphs: Amgen Inc. (AMGN) is an S&P 500 constituent with established competitive advantages and consistent cash flow generation. The company's market position provides pricing power and earnings stability.

My DCF applies a 6% FCF growth rate reflecting the company's historical compounding ability, with a 8.5% discount rate capturing sector-specific risks. The valuation is sensitive to growth assumptions — investors should stress-test these inputs.
verdictFooter: Opus 4.6 independent analysis. Data via public filings (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: What is Amgen Inc.'s intrinsic value according to Opus?
faq.answer: Based on a 10-year DCF with 6% growth and 8.5% WACC, Opus values AMGN at $459.48/share vs $310.00 current price.

faq.question: Is AMGN overvalued or undervalued?
faq.answer: Opus's DCF suggests AMGN is undervalued with a +48.2% margin of safety.

faq.question: Should I trade based on this?
faq.answer: No. This is not financial advice. DCF models are sensitive to assumptions. Use as one data point among many.

prospectVerdictDetail: Amgen's legacy biologics face biosimilar competition but the obesity pipeline (MariTide) could be transformative. The Horizon Therapeutics acquisition adds rare disease revenue. Valuation is reasonable but growth depends on unproven pipeline drugs.
prospectKeyRisks: Biosimilar erosion of legacy drugs (Enbrel, Prolia) compressing base business revenue
MariTide obesity drug clinical data failing to match GLP-1 competitors on efficacy
Horizon Therapeutics integration failing to deliver revenue synergies
prospectKeyCatalysts: MariTide (monthly injection) achieving competitive weight loss efficacy, entering the $100B+ GLP-1 market
Biosimilar business generating $5B+ revenue by selling competitors' biosimilar versions
Rare disease portfolio from Horizon growing 20%+ with limited competition
prospectMethodology: Score is based on three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30), totaling 0-100. Each pillar is broken into individually scored factors with transparent rationale. Data sources include FY2025 10-K filings, analyst consensus estimates, news sentiment analysis, and competitive landscape assessment. The score is forward-looking and represents economic prospect over a 2-3 year horizon.
